title: Interactive Study Companion
description: Question bank and reference links live together in one grade file so the static app can load everything with one fetch.

chapters.title: New Testament 2: Epistles of St. Paul
St. Mary - The Handmaid of God
History of the Syriac Orthodox Church

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When did Jesus establish the Holy Qurbono as the New Testament sacrifice? What was replaced?
questions.answer: On the day of the Last Supper. Animal sacrifice of the Old Testament.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Holy Qurbono commemorate?
questions.answer: Birth, public ministry, death, resurrection, and Second Coming of Jesus Christ.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the word 'Qurbono' mean?
questions.answer: Offering of sacrifice.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is another name for Holy Qurbono/Holy Communion?
questions.answer: Queen of Sacraments.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Holy Qurbono is the '________'
questions.answer: Bread of life.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 4 components of the Holy Qurbono?
questions.answer: The celebrant, The Sacramental items, Book of Holy Liturgy (Anaphora/Qurbono Taksa), and Recipient (congregation).

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who is the celebrant and who is the ambassador?
questions.answer: God is the celebrant, and the Prelate (Patriarch/Catholicose/bishop) or priest is the ambassador.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the primary Sacramental items for the holy Qurbono?
questions.answer: Leavened bread and wine mixed with water.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the official terms for the Book/Rite of the Holy Qurbono?
questions.answer: Anaphora or Qurbono Taksa.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many different Taksas are there in existence?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The first church in Christianity is also known as _________. What occurred here?
questions.answer: Upper Room of St. Mark. The Last Supper occurred here.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who consecrates a church building? What is it anointed with?
questions.answer: A bishop. It is anointed with Holy Chrism (Mooron).

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the church building a symbol of?
questions.answer: The presence of God among us.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 3 main divisions of a church building?
questions.answer: Holy of the holies (the Sanctuary of the Madbaho), Chancel or Kestrumo, and Nave or the Common Place.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Madbaho is from the root word dbah, meaning _________
questions.answer: To sacrifice.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Madbaho is the most sacred place in the church building. What does it represent?
questions.answer: The Golgotha where the sacrifice of our Lord took place.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the thronos?
questions.answer: The altar table located in the center of the sanctuary.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Chancel/Kestrumo represent? What was offered here and when?
questions.answer: The space between the valley and the place where Moses prayed. Animal sacrifices in the Old Testament times were offered here.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Nave/Common Place represent?
questions.answer: The valley where the Israelites waited to receive blessing from God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the veil called? What does it represent when it is opened?
questions.answer: Thirasheela. It represents the opening of heaven.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does thronos mean? What does it represent? What is another name for it?
questions.answer: It means Throne. It represents the tomb of Christ and is also called the Table of Life.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Sleebo mean? Where is it placed? What does it symbolize? It is described as the _________
questions.answer: Cross. Placed in the middle of the thronos. It symbolizes the Lord's sacrifice on Mount Golgotha, and it is described as the weapon in the fight against Satan.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the 12 candles represent? What does the light from the candles symbolize?
questions.answer: The 12 apostles. The light symbolizes the presence of God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Candles made of animal fats permitted to use in the church. True or False?
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Tablitho? Why should it be handled carefully by a bishop, priest, or deacon? What does it have an inscription of?
questions.answer: A tablet/piece of wooden slab placed on the center of the thronos and covered with a cloth. It should be handled carefully because it is anointed with Mooron. It has an inscription detailing the place, date, and by whom the consecration was done.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is another name for the Tablitho? Why is it called this?
questions.answer: Temporary altar. It is called this because Holy Qurbono can be celebrated anywhere with it.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Without the Tablitho the Qurbono cannot be celebrated. True or False?
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Tablitho symbolize?
questions.answer: The cross where Jesus was crucified.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many times can the Qurbono be celebrated on one Tablitho per day?
questions.answer: Once

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is placed on the Tablitho during the Holy Qurbono?
questions.answer: The paten and chalice.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the tricolor spread called? Describe it and what the colors represent.
questions.answer: Virikootam. It covers the Tablitho and is made of red, green, and white/yellow pieces of cloth. Red represents the universe, Green represents the world, and White/Yellow represents the Holy Church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the altar cloth called? What do the three parts symbolize?
questions.answer: Chithola. The three parts symbolize the Holy Trinity.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the altar step called? When does the celebrant stand on it and what does it symbolize?
questions.answer: Dargo. The celebrant stands on it during the Anaphora (after the Nicene Creed). It symbolizes Christ's entrance into the Upper Room of St. Mark for the Last Supper.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the chalice and paten called? What do they hold?
questions.answer: Kasa and Peelaso. Kasa holds the wine and Peelaso holds the bread.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the purpose of the Kablana? When is it not used?
questions.answer: Used to cover the chalice and paten at the beginning and close of the service. It is not used when the chalice and paten are covered by the Shushefo.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Kablana symbolizes 2 things. What are they?
questions.answer: The shroud that covered the body of Christ & the clouds that covered the tent in the desert during the plight of the Israelites from Egypt.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Star (cross) called? Where is it placed? What does it symbolize?
questions.answer: Kaukbo. It is placed over the paten and symbolizes the Star of Nativity.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the bowl called? What is it used for?
questions.answer: Mshamshonitho. It is used to mix the wine with water & for the priest to clean his fingers when handling the Holy Body and Blood.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the spoon called? What does it represent?
questions.answer: Tharvodo. It represents the tongs that the angel used in giving the glowing coal to the mouth of prophet Isaiah in his vision.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the cushion called? What is it used for? What does it symbolize?
questions.answer: Gmurto. It is used to clean the Sacramental vessels. It symbolizes the sponge used by the soldier to offer the sour wine to Christ while He was on the Cross.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Shushefo used for? What does it represent?
questions.answer: To cover the paten and chalice. It represents the fact that the Divine Mysteries are hidden from the understanding of men.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Shushefo symbolizes 2 things. What are they?
questions.answer: The stone rolled over the Tomb of Christ & the rock of flint that sent 12 streams of water for the 12 tribes of Israel.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Lectern? What does it symbolize? It is honored as the second _________
questions.answer: Gospel Stand or Evengelion table. It symbolizes the heights of Mount Sinai where God spoke to Moses and handed him the Ten Commandments. It is honored as the second Thronos.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Censer called? How many chains and bells?
questions.answer: Dhoopakutty. It has 4 chains and 12 bells.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the lower and upper cups of the censer represent? (Note: the lower cup represents 2 things)
questions.answer: The lower cup represents the earth and the womb of St. Mary. The upper cup represents heaven.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does each chain of the censer represent?
questions.answer: 1st chain = God the Father; 2nd and 3rd chain = human and divine nature of God; 4th chain = The Holy Spirit.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the bells of the censer represent?
questions.answer: The 12 apostles.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the links on the chains of the censer represent?
questions.answer: The saints, prophets, and emissaries who led the people of God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the coal, fire, and incense of the censer represent?
questions.answer: Coal represents the sinners; Fire represents the Holy Spirit purifying the sinners to become children of God; Incense represents the grace of the Holy Trinity and the cleansing of sins.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the altar assistants use the kai mani for?
questions.answer: To proclaim and draw attention of the congregation to the important parts of the Qurbono.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Church Bell used for and when? What should we do when we hear it ring?
questions.answer: To assemble the congregation to the Church for worship services and alerts people outside the church that the Holy Qurbono is about to start. We should make the sign of the cross and say 'Bless my Lord'.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Once the Holy Qurbono begins, the big Church bell should not ring. True or False?
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: There are 3 other times when the Church bell is rung. What are they?
questions.answer: At the arrival of a bishop, during the procession for festivals, and when a dead body is brought to the church for the funeral.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the fans called? What do they symbolize? What does their sound represent?
questions.answer: Marvahtho. They symbolize the presence of angels around the altar. Their sound represents the seraphim and the fluttering of their wings around the mysteries.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Lahmo? What is it made of?
questions.answer: Eucharistic bread. It is made of wheat, flour, pure water, salt, leaven, and olive oil.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The participants need to spend time in _________, _________ and _________ in order to be filled with thankfulness and repentance.
questions.answer: Prayer, Meditation, Reading the Holy scriptures

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Without _________ we cannot get reconciled with God and dedicate ourselves to God's will.
questions.answer: Reconciliation with our brethren

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 4 types of abstinence we should practice while preparing for the Qurbono?
questions.answer: Avoid worldly things and concentrate on Divine, Fasting, Physical Abstinence, and Observance of Silence.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When should the preparation for Holy Communion begin?
questions.answer: On the previous evening.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When should you begin to fast if you plan to receive Holy Communion?
questions.answer: From midnight till you receive the Holy Communion.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What should you refrain from in order to prepare in fasting and prayer?
questions.answer: All aspects of sexual activity.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Per the Canons of the Holy Church, we are advised to not talk to _________ before attending the Holy Qurbono. Hence it is better practice to _________ as far as possible.
questions.answer: Non-Christians, Observe silence

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the name of the prayer the celebrant offers to those that are coming to receive Holy Communion?
questions.answer: Hoosoyo

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: (In reference to the answer from #63) It replaces the Sacrament of Holy Confession. True or False?
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why do we take our shoes off before entering the Church?
questions.answer: Because it is in reverence to the holiness of the place we are entering.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the prayer we say when we enter the Church?
questions.answer: "With reverence will I enter thy house and offer my vows to Thee" (Psalm 61:8).

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When is the dough for the Eucharistic bread prepared? When is it baked?
questions.answer: Prepared on the eve of the Holy Qurbono, and baked on the same day of the Qurbono.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the name of the set of preparatory prayers before the Holy Qurbono when the sanctuary is concealed by the veil?
questions.answer: Thuyobo

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 2 orders of priesthood, per the Holy Bible?
questions.answer: The Order of Melchizedeck and the Order of Aaron.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How is Malchizedeck described in the Bible?
questions.answer: As the king of Salem and the priest of the most high God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is it called when the priest turns to the congregation and asks for forgiveness and prayers to make him worthy to offer the living sacrifice? When is the first time he does this?
questions.answer: Shubkono. The first time he does this is during the Order of Melchizedeck (during morning prayer when he enters the Madbaho for the first time).

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: After the priest places the bread in paten, the chalice is prepared with equal water and wine. What does this represent?
questions.answer: The flow of blood and water from the side of Jesus when a soldier pierced His side while on the cross.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Order of Aaron symbolize?
questions.answer: The offerings of Aaron and his sons as mentioned in Leviticus 1.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When the celebrant covers the paten and chalice with the Shushefo, he censes the mysteries 3 times at the center, 3 times on the left, and 3 times on the right. When he censes on the left, he _________
questions.answer: Folds the corner of the Shushefo saying 'Son died on the cross'.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How is the Order of Aaron concluded?
questions.answer: With the huthomo, quamo, and the Nicene Creed.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the names of the 2 parts of the Public Celebration of the Holy Qurbono?
questions.answer: Mass of the Catechumens and the Anaphora of the faithful.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: As part of the Holy Qurbono, the Church provides the opportunity for special prayers. Name the 4 categories of special prayers. When are they remembered?
questions.answer: Vazhipadkurbana, intercession, sickness, and departed. They are remembered during the Thuyobo.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was the Mass of the Catechumens primarily intended for?
questions.answer: New believers and those who were not yet baptized.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Mass of the Catechumens consist of?
questions.answer: Procession around the thronos, the Trisagion, the reading from the epistle, and the Gospel and sermon.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the opening of the Thirasheela represent at the start of the Public Celebration?
questions.answer: The birth of the New Testament age and opening of heaven to the faithful.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who do the celebrant and deacon/thurifer holding the candle represent when they move in a procession around the thronos? (after "Mariyam Dheelethok...")
questions.answer: Christ the High Priest and John the Baptist who prepared the way for our Lord.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does this procession signify? What does the censing around the altar symbolize?
questions.answer: The public ministry of our Lord across the 4 corners of the earth. The censing around the altar symbolizes the purification of the altar.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who heard the angels sing the Trisagion and when/where?
questions.answer: Nicodemus and Joseph of Aramathia. They heard it when they took the body of Christ for burial / during the crucifixion.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: During the lections (Bible readings) the deacon/altar server steps down to the chancel step. What does this and the readings at the north and south sides symbolize?
questions.answer: The fact that the Apostles were sent by heavenly authority and the fact that they spread the Gospel in all corners of the world.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the word 'Habibai' mean?
questions.answer: My beloved.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the word 'Ahai' mean?
questions.answer: My brethren.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the lit candles during the Gospel reading represent?
questions.answer: The Cherubim with flaming swords.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the rites included in the Pre-Anaphora?
questions.answer: Promion, Hoosoyo, Sedro, blessing of the censer, and the Nicene Creed.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does 'Staumen kalos' mean?
questions.answer: Let us stand well OR be attentive.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does 'Kuriyelaison' mean?
questions.answer: Lord have mercy upon us.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the importance of the Promion?
questions.answer: It remembers the great sacrifice of our Lord and asks him to make us worthy of offering the sacrifice.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: After the Promion, the celebrant places incense and unfolds the left corner of the Shushefo. What does he say when he does this and what does it symbolize?
questions.answer: "Christ risen from the tomb". It symbolizes the revelation of the resurrected Christ to humanity.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What prayer follows the Promion? What is the importance of it?
questions.answer: Hoosoyo. The importance is that the celebrant asks the Lord for mercy and absolution on behalf of the congregation.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the long prayer reflecting on the fundamental tenets of our faith and on the greatness of the divine Qurbono called?
questions.answer: Sedro

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The blessing of the censer is also a _________
questions.answer: Confession of the Holy Trinity.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe which part of the Qurbono the celebrant asks for Shubkono for the SECOND time.
questions.answer: After the blessing of the censer / during the Nicene Creed / before the Anaphora.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When was the Nicene Creed formulated, where, and by whom?
questions.answer: In 325 AD at the Council of Nicea, by the 318 Fathers of the early Church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Nicene Creed was formulated after the Church determined the New Testament. True or False?
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the major tenets of the Nicene Creed?
questions.answer: Holy Trinity, Holy Church, Baptism, and Final resurrection.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 4 fundamental characteristics of the Holy Church, as written in the Nicene Creed?
questions.answer: One, Holy, Catholic, and Apostolic.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the term 'Anaphora' mean?
questions.answer: Offering

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the celebrant do that signifies the start of the Anaphora?
questions.answer: He steps on to the Dargo, representing the Lord's entrance into the Upper Room of St. Mark for the Last Supper.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did the Church adopt the tradition of the Kiss of Peace?
questions.answer: From the Jewish practice of kissing/hugging one another before a meal.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Kiss of Peace also fulfills the words of our Lord from His _________
questions.answer: Sermon on the Mount

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The celebration of the Shushefo is symbolic of _________'s vision at the city of Joppa. What did the vision tell this person?
questions.answer: St. Peter. It told him that the grace of God is open to all who confess their faith in Christ.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the celebration of the Shushefo also represent? (Hint: Jesus' baptism)
questions.answer: The opening of heaven and the descent of the Holy Spirit in the form of a dove.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the first benediction also known as?
questions.answer: The Apostolic Benediction.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the most important part of the Qurbono known as?
questions.answer: The Words of Institution.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the meaning of 'a'damo doseno'?
questions.answer: Until I come.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When the celebrant says 'a'damo doseno,' he raises the Tharvodo and Gmurto over his right shoulder in a quick motion. What does this represent?
questions.answer: The second coming of Christ on the last day, which will be like a flash of lightning.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the celebrant asking for during the Invocation of the Holy Spirit?
questions.answer: That God the Father would send down the Holy Spirit upon the bread and wine.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When the celebrant raises his hands and cries out 'Answer me, O Lord' 3x, it is similar to which other invocation from the Bible?
questions.answer: Prophet Elijah's at Mount Carmel.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why is the Invocation of the Holy Spirit also another most important part of the Qurbono?
questions.answer: Because this is when the bread and wine truly become the body and blood of Christ.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the word 'thubden' mean?
questions.answer: Again

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who do the first 3 thubdens remember? Name them all.
questions.answer: The living. They remember the spiritual fathers who tend the Church, the living faithful brethren, and the living faithful rulers.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who do the last 3 thubdens remember? Name them all.
questions.answer: The departed. They remember the intercession of the Mother of God and saints, the fathers of the Church, and the departed faithful.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the importance of the second benediction?
questions.answer: The blessing is by God our Saviour Jesus Christ instead of the Holy Trinity as in the other 2 benedictions.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: During the song 'Anpudayone', what does the closed veil remind us of?
questions.answer: The time of Christ's redemptive passion, death, burial, and resurrection, when the earth was engulfed in darkness.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the importance of the Fracture & Commixture?
questions.answer: This is the part where the celebrant commemorates the passion, crucifixion, and resurrection of our Lord.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: During 'Anpudayone', we hear the bells being rung. What is the significance?
questions.answer: The resurrection of Christ.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 3 different patterns of breaking the bread? Explain each one.
questions.answer: Lamb pattern: used from Koodash Etho to Easter, Maundy Thursday, and Holy Saturday. Crucifix Pattern: human; used from Easter to the Feast of the Holy Cross. Cross Pattern: used from the Feast of the Cross to Koodash Etho.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: After the Fracture & Commixture, what is the importance of the veil being opened?
questions.answer: This part corresponds to the days between the resurrection and ascension. It shows that the Old Testament priesthood, which was restricted to one tribe, was opened to all through the sacrifice of Christ, revealed through the tearing of the veil in the Jerusalem Temple. Man was brought close to God again, where they could call God 'Father'.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the third benediction symbolize?
questions.answer: The blessing that our Lord gave to His disciples before His ascension.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Elevation of the Holy Mysteries celebrate?
questions.answer: The ascension of our Lord.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Afterwards, the celebrant covers places the paten and chalice on the Tablitho and covers them with the Kablana. What does this represent?
questions.answer: The Kablana represents the cloud that hid Christ during His ascension.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When is the THIRD time the celebrant asks for Shubkono?
questions.answer: After the Elevation of the Holy Mysteries and during the Hymns of Eucharistic Devotion.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What song can the Hymns of Eucharistic devotion be replaced with?
questions.answer: "Bakyamthe d'malko..."

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 4 categories for the Hymns of Eucharistic devotion?
questions.answer: Intercession of St. Mary, Intercession of the Saints, Prayers for the departed Clergy, and Prayers for the departed faithful.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Each category has 4 songs. What are the Syrian names/titles of these songs?
questions.answer: Quaqal'yon (Kukkilion), Eqbo, Kolo, and Boovoso.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Before the Procession of the Holy Mysteries, the veil is closed. What does this symbolize?
questions.answer: The age in which the Church awaits the second coming of our Lord.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: At the start of the Procession of the Holy Mysteries, the veil is opened. What does this represent?
questions.answer: The glorious second coming of our Lord.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the significance of the celebrant turning counterclockwise to face the congregation?
questions.answer: That the Lord is coming as a judge this time, which is in contrast of the first coming when He came as Redeemer.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When the celebrant stretches out his hands to introduce the Holy Body ad Holy Blood one after the other, he pleads to the Lord that the reception of the Holy Mysteries be for _________ and _________ and not for any _________ or _________
questions.answer: Life, Redemption, Punishment, Condemnation

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the purpose of the Prayer of Thanksgiving?
questions.answer: For the celebrant to offer praise and express thanks to the Father for making him and the congregation worthy to partake in the heavenly feast, which is like a joyful wedding banquet.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When the celebrant greets the congregation with peace for the last time, it is representing _________
questions.answer: The eternal peace offered to the holy people at the Second Coming of our Lord.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the name of the concluding prayer done by the celebrant?
questions.answer: Hoothomo

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: After the benediction, the celebrant steps down and kneels before the thronos for a silent prayer. The veil is drawn to close the sanctuary. What is the significance of this?
questions.answer: It denotes the conclusion of the Public Celebration of the Holy Qurbono.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why is the final benediction so important?
questions.answer: It acknowledges the presence of all who are 'far and near', 'living and departed', 'who are saved by the victorious cross' and 'who are inscribed with the seal of Holy Baptism' during the Holy Qurbono and that the blessing is for all those who are participating in it physically or spiritually.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: After the post-communion prayers, how does the celebrant take leave of the altar?
questions.answer: By stepping down from the Dargo and kissing the Tablitho to the middle to the left and to the right saying the three prayers of farewell with a contrite heart.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Name the Ten Commandments.
questions.answer: You shall have no other gods before me. You shall not make for yourself a graven image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above or that is in earth beneath, or that is in the water underneath the earth. You shall not bow down to them or serve them. You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain. Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy. Honor your mother and father, that your days may be long in the land which the Lord your God gives you. You shall not kill. You shall not commit adultery. You shall not steal. You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor. You shall not covet your neighbor's house or anything that is your neighbor's.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 7 Laws of the Holy Church?
questions.answer: Attend the Holy Qurbono on Sundays and other obligatory days. Observe the 5 Lents of the Church. Observe the Lent on Wednesdays and Fridays. Confess the sins before a canonical priest. Receive the Holy Communion. Abstain from marital relationship on prescribed days. Pay offerings, tithes, and duties to the church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did the 3 Ecumenical Synods take place (and the importance of each--just the overall importance)?
questions.answer: Nicea: Father and Son. Constantinople: Holy Spirit. Ephesus: Holy Virgin Mother.
