questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Prayer before receiving Holy Communion
questions.answer: My God and my Lord, make me worthy to receive Thy Holy Body and life-giving precious blood for the eradication of my evil desires, and for the expulsion of sinful deeds. Help me O God, through partaking in these Holy Mysteries, for the remission of my debts, and forgiveness of my sins; and for the purification of my body and the enlightenment of my soul into life everlasting. Amen.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Prayer after receiving Holy Communion
questions.answer: O Lord Jesus Christ, our God, I have received Thy Holy Body and precious Blood unto remission of sins and for life eternal. May this Eucharist be unto me not for condemnation, but for the life and redemption; for joy, health, and gladness; and to make me worthy to stand at Thy right hand during Thy second coming. Amen.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the books of the Torah?
questions.answer: Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, Deuteronomy

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many Apocryphal deuterocanonical books has our church officially accepted?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did the first Holy Synod convene to make the first unanimous decision on the Canon of the Bible?
questions.answer: Carthage, Africa

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What were the 6 standards through which the 27 books of the New Testament were chosen?
questions.answer: Apostolic, Dogmas, Christocentric, Inspirational, Witness of Holy Spirit, Early Acceptance

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the official name of the Syriac Orthodox Bible in Malayalam?
questions.answer: Vishudha Grandham

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Explain the role of the Holy Synod in the process of Canonization of the Bible.
questions.answer: The Holy Synods decided which books to include in the Bible and became the final word of the Canon. The Holy Fathers who attended the Synods carefully examined the authority of each book under the guidance of the Holy Spirit.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Muratorian Fragment, and why is it significant?
questions.answer: The Muratorian Fragment is the oldest known list of New Testament Books, though its beginning is missing. It is significant because most of the books mentioned became part of the Bible later, while some books in today’s New Testament were not included, showing the early Church’s process of forming the canon.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Septuagint mean in Latin?
questions.answer: Seventy

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many sections are the books of the Septuagint divided into?
questions.answer:
Four main sections
1) Books of the Law: Genesis to Deuteronomy
2) Books of Israel’s History: Joshua to Maccabees
3) Books of Wisdom: Psalms to Wisdom of Sirach
4) Books of Prophecy: Hosea to Daniel

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Books of Law known as?
questions.answer: Pentateuch or Torah

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote the Torah?
questions.answer: Prophet Moses

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe what Genesis means and its focus.
questions.answer: Genesis means “beginning,” and it describes God’s creation of the world and the fall of man, beginning with Adam and Eve.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the meaning of Exodus?
questions.answer: Departure

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the book of Leviticus detail?
questions.answer: The worship, as dictated by God, to be led by ordained priests from the tribe of Levi.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the book of Numbers about?
questions.answer: The Israelites' 40-year journey from Mount Sinai to Canaan, focusing on their life in the wilderness, their disobedience, and the Lord’s protection over them.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the meaning of Deuteronomy?
questions.answer: Second Law

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What happens in Deuteronomy?
questions.answer: Moses repeats God’s laws to the Israelites before his death on the eastern side of the Jordan in the land of Moab.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Joshua’s name mean?
questions.answer: Salvation

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What happened during the Battle at Gibeon?
questions.answer: The Lord made huge hailstones fall from the sky and crush the enemy soldiers.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who is believed to have written the book of Judges?
questions.answer: Samuel

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Ruth’s relation to Christ?
questions.answer: Through the marriage of Boaz, Ruth is the great-grandmother of King David and the foremother of Jesus.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Summarize the book of Ruth.
questions.answer: Ruth was a Gentile woman who refused to leave her mother-in-law, Naomi alone after the death of her husband. She placed herself under the protection of the one true God and was redeemed by Boaz, who then became her husband. Ruth became the great-grandmother of King David and the foremother of Jesus.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was the first King of Israel?
questions.answer: Saul

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote the First and Second Kingdoms?
questions.answer: Samuel

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Third and Fourth Kingdoms known as in the Old Testament?
questions.answer: 1 and 2 Kings

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote the First and Second Chronicles?
questions.answer: Ezra

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: During what time was First and Second Ezra written?
questions.answer: During the time of the Israelites' return to Jerusalem from their captivity in Babylon.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was Nehemiah?
questions.answer: A common man who was faithful to God. He shaped the religious practices of the Jewish community after their return from exile in Babylon.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Tobit about?
questions.answer: A fervent believer in exile in Nineveh

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the major theme of Judith?
questions.answer: Judith’s unfailing trust in God to protect her people

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How did Esther use her royal position?
questions.answer: To persuade the king to save her people from the evil plans of a general named Haman, who was determined to eradicate the Jews.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the major theme of Maccabees?
questions.answer: God’s deliverance of Israel during the Jewish wars and how he rewards those who are faithful to the Law and punishes the evildoers.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many books are in the books of wisdom?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many hymns are in Psalms according to the Septuagint Old Testament?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote the Psalms?
questions.answer: David, Moses, and the temple musicians

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the themes found in Psalms?
questions.answer: Prophecies of the coming Messiah, prayers for the king, personal lamentations, and songs of praise and hymns for special feast.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the book of Job about?
questions.answer: Job’s unshakeable faith in the face of tragedy and innocent suffering

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Which book is a collection of moral and religious instructions taught to young people after their return to Israel from exile?
questions.answer: Proverbs

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the two apocryphal books in the books of wisdom?
questions.answer: Wisdom of Solomon and Wisdom of Sirach

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The character of Job can be equated to that of who in the Bible, who suffers unjustly and is rewarded for his obedience (Philippians 2:9-11).
questions.answer: Jesus

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: King Solomon is considered to be the main writer of what books?
questions.answer: Song of Songs, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote the wisdom of Solomon?
questions.answer: A Jewish believer in Egypt

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Prophet Hosea teach?
questions.answer: He describes the love of God as being steadfast and whole, even when his chosen people ignore His Laws.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Hosea commonly referred to as?
questions.answer: The prophet of love

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Micah preach?
questions.answer: Peace for those who do justice, love kindness, and walk humbly with God

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the name “Amos” mean?
questions.answer: Burden bearer

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the Wisdom of Sirach also known as, and what book is it similar to?
questions.answer: It is also known as Ecclesiastes, and is written similarly to Proverbs

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Prophet Joel known as?
questions.answer: Prophet of Pentecost

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Which book is the shortest of the prophetic books of the Holy Bible?
questions.answer: Obadiah

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does “Jonah” mean?
questions.answer: A dove

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the main theme of the book of Jonah?
questions.answer: Universality of God’s love and mercy

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the meaning of Nahum's name?
questions.answer: Consolidation

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who is the first post-exile prophet?
questions.answer: Haggai

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who is the second of the post-exile prophets who prophesied Christ’s humble coming?
questions.answer: Zechariah

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Prophet Malachi prophesize?
questions.answer: The birth of a forerunner, like the ancient prophet Elijah, to appear before the bird of the Messiah.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was Baruch?
questions.answer: The secretary of Prophet Jeremiah

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When was the Lamentations of Jeremiah written?
questions.answer: After the destruction of Jerusalem

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does “Ezekiel” mean?
questions.answer: God strengthens

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who among the major prophets described the new covenant, and what did his book foretell?
questions.answer: Jeremiah prophesied the “new covenant” between God and mankind to be fulfilled through the Messiah of Israel. He foretells the capture of Jews by the Babylonians and the destruction of the temple in Jerusalem.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did Moses receive the 10 Commandments from God?
questions.answer: Mount Sinai

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was Moses’ successor as the leader of the Israelites?
questions.answer: Joshua

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was Moses when he breathed his last breath?
questions.answer: Mount Nebo

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why did New Testament writers compare Moses?
questions.answer: To explain Jesus’ mission

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: Moses along with Elijah, is presented as meeting with Jesus in all three Gospel accounts of the Transfiguration of Jesus.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Jesus refer to the scribes and the Pharisees of the Temple as?
questions.answer: “Seated in the chair of Moses”

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many days did Moses lead the Israelites through the Shur Desert?
questions.answer: 3 days

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did Moses and the Israelites find water?
questions.answer: Marah

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Moses throw into the water so that the Israelites could drink it?
questions.answer: A piece of wood

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many years were the Exodus?
questions.answer: 40 years

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What food did God provide to the Israelites?
questions.answer: Manna, bread from the heavens

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many years was Moses alive?
questions.answer: 120 years

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Moses’ life begin with?
questions.answer: The faith of his parents

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Everything Moses did after he was old enough to understand the plight of his people was with what?
questions.answer: Faith

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: Despite the hardened heart of the Pharaoh, Moses continued to make his way into Pharaoh’s presence and ask for the Israelites’ release.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: In all the accounts of Moses’ books, what is one thing that echoed throughout the book?
questions.answer: The people complained about things constantly

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Throughout Moses’ journey leading the people of Israel, what did he constantly have to deal with?
questions.answer: The Israelites rebelling against him and God

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How did Moses deal with difficult situations?
questions.answer: Pleaded to God on behalf of the people

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: As a leader, Moses was impatient with the Israelites.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: Moses was always obedient to God’s command.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was Moses the first forty years of his life?
questions.answer: Egypt

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was Moses the second forty years of his life?
questions.answer: Land of Midian

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was Moses the final forty years of his life?
questions.answer: Leading the children of Israel through the wilderness

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the qualities God gave to Moses to face the hardships?
questions.answer: Faith in God, patience and persistence, obedience, humility

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was Jesus seen talking with the Prophets Elijah and Moses?
questions.answer: Mount Tabor

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do Moses and Elijah represent?
questions.answer: Moses represents the law, while Elijah represents the prophets.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does faith provide?
questions.answer: Faith gives people the weapon necessary to overcome even the toughest situations, as well as the strength to persevere and pursue.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How were the lives of Moses and Jesus alike?
questions.answer:
Jesus’ escape from the slaughter by Herod in Bethlehem is compared to Moses’ escape from Pharaoh’s designs to kill Hebrew infants.
Forty days of fasting prior to Moses’ starting his journey with the Israelites is similar to Christ’s fasting in the wilderness before his public ministry.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What responsibilities were given to Moses?
questions.answer: He was responsible for setting rules, solving altercations among people, providing for all the people and their animals, taking care of the safety of his people, and maintaining the faith of the people in the one true God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many books are in the New Testament?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the names of the three parts of the New Testament?
questions.answer: Gospels and Acts, Epistles, and Apocalyptic Text

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the two types of Epistles?
questions.answer: Pauline and General

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Gospel mean?
questions.answer: Good News

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Gospel present Jesus as?
questions.answer: A fulfillment of the Old Testament prophecies and Law

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Matthew emphasize in his chapter?
questions.answer: Jesus as the Messiah who fulfills the Old Testament Scriptures

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does St. Mark’s Gospel emphasize?
questions.answer: The omnipotent power of Jesus that shows His Divinity through his miracles

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was St. Luke?
questions.answer: A disciple of St. Paul

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does St. Luke’s Gospel emphasize?
questions.answer: The humanity of Jesus covering His birth and His genealogy

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What was the special emphasis in St. John’s Gospel?
questions.answer: To demonstrate to the world that Jesus was the Son of God

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote Acts?
questions.answer: St. Luke

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the first part of Acts of Apostle about?
questions.answer: Narrates the spread of the Gospel in Palestine, Judea, and Samaria by Peter, James, and John

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the second part of Acts of Apostle about?
questions.answer: Describes the work of Apostle Paul as a missionary to the Gentiles

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What was James about?
questions.answer: Focuses on several practical topics related to Christian living, including Christian wisdom, faith and works, and discipline in speech

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When was the First and Second Epistles of Peter written?
questions.answer: A time of severe Christian persecution

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the first Epistle of Peter about?
questions.answer: A call to God’s people drawing a link between Christ’s suffering and us, who fulfill His work through suffering

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the second Epistle of Peter about?
questions.answer: Warns against false teachers and instead focuses on the True Gospel

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was Judas?
questions.answer: The brother of Jesus

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who wrote Revelation
questions.answer: John

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why is the Book of Revelation not in our Church lectionary?
questions.answer: It is considered a difficult text to read because of its rich imagery and symbolism regarding things we may not understand.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe why the Gospel of John is unique among the four Gospels.
questions.answer: There is an intentional effort by St. John to minimize the overlap of this account compared to the first three gospels. He makes a special emphasis to demonstrate to the world that Jesus was the Son of God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who were the two most narrated Apostles in the book of Acts and why were their efforts important?
questions.answer: St. Peter and St. Paul, who spread the gospel to both jews and apostles

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Explain what each of John’s epistles was about.
questions.answer:
1) First Epistle: Intended to promote brotherly love, warn against doctrine that allowed men to live in sin, and to provide clear direction on the nature of God and the divine glory of Jesus Christ
2) Second Epistle: A personal letter written to a pious lady to urge her to love God and also those who loved Him, and to avoid false teaching and hold fast to her the true Christian faith
3) Third Epistle: Compares two people to Gaius and urges him to imitate the one who is respected for his behavior as a good Christian

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was St. Paul born?
questions.answer: Cilicia

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was St. Paul?
questions.answer: An Apostle, Pharisee, and Descendant from the tribe of Benjamin

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many Missionary Journeys did St. Paul make?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How long did St. Paul’s first missionary journey last?
questions.answer: 18 months

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did St. Paul start his second missionary journey?
questions.answer: Antioch

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What was the aim of St. Paul’s third missionary journey?
questions.answer: To see the growth of the Church which he already preached

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did St. Paul do in Ephesus during his third missionary journey?
questions.answer: He healed many people with his dress or handkerchief

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why was St. Paul forced to end his third missionary journey?
questions.answer: Due to the silversmith riots

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did the Jews accuse him of in his final journey?
questions.answer: He was accused of preaching against Mosaic Law

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: In the New Testament, how many letters are ascribed to St. Paul?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many letters were addressed to communities?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many letters were addressed to individuals?

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How did St. Paul die?
questions.answer: He was beheaded by the order of Emperor Nero

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where did St. Paul spend 3 years after his conversion to prepare himself for God through divine visions?
questions.answer: The desert of Arabia

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What were the two names of St. Paul and what are their meanings?
questions.answer: The religious Hebrew name ‘Saul’, meaning “desired” or “asked for”, and the Roman name ‘Paul’, meaning “small.”

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: List the themes found within the Pauline epistles.
questions.answer: Church: The Body of Christ, Discipline of Church, Priesthood, Holy Eucharist, Marital Life, Suffering, Resurrection of the Dead

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe St. Paul’s importance in the Church.
questions.answer: St. Paul was important to the Church because he led four missionary journeys that spread Christianity after being directly called upon by God. He also wrote 14 letters (epistles) that explain Christian beliefs, teachings, and guidance for the Church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who were St. Mary’s parents?
questions.answer: Joachim and Hanna

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Which angel came down from heaven to impart to St. Mary the good news?
questions.answer: Angel Gabriel

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the name Mary mean?
questions.answer: Sea of bitterness

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How did the priests of the temple know that St. Joseph was to be the husband of St. Mary?
questions.answer: A dove flew up and stood on the staff belonging to St. Joseph

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Which diptych honors St. Mary?
questions.answer: The 4th diptych

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why was St. Mary consecrated to the temple at the age of three (until she was 12)?
questions.answer: Her parents vowed that if God gave them a child, the child would be a servant in the Temple of God forever.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: List and shortly describe the 7 virtues of St. Mary.
questions.answer:
1) Prayerful: St. Mary joined the disciples in prayer and fellowship, true and faithful to the One who chose her.
2) Chaste: St. Mary remained pure, never straying from the will of God
3) Prudent: Instead of boasting that her son was an extraordinary boy, she kept these things and pondered them in her heart.
4) Humble: St. Mary was born into a poor family and she continued to live an ordinary life even though she bore the creator of the world
5) Faithful: She stood by her son until death, enduring the pain with the grace that came with the trust in God’s almighty plan
6) Obedient: She did everything the law of the Lord commanded without any protest
7) Spiritual Strength: Even through hardships, Mary never uttered a word against God or questioned whatever happened in her life.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What symbolizes Virgin Mary?
questions.answer: The Arc of Covenant, which kept the law in two tablets, and the vessel that kept manna.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe the three Orthodox teachings about St. Mary.
questions.answer:
1) St. Mary is the Ever Virgin: St. Mary remained a virgin before birth, at birth, and after the birth of Jesus.
2) St. Mary is the Mother of God: Is it through her flesh and the Holy Spirit that Jesus was incarnate as fully man and fully God.
3) St. Mary was taken into Heaven (Assumption of St. Mary): Jesus Christ honored his mother by giving her an assumption to heaven with respect and glory.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Divine tradition is that teaching given by whom?
questions.answer: Given by Jesus Christ directly and by word of mouth to the Apostles

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: ________  is the teachings of Christ which was orally communicated to the Apostles.
questions.answer: Apostolic tradition

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Apostolic tradition include?
questions.answer: The teachings of the Apostles, their laws, and the messages they handed down to their disciples and their immediate successors. It also includes constitutions of faith, the Church Sacraments, and enacted laws and liturgies derived from Christ and established by the Apostles.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: _________ is what the Holy Church has received from the dawn of its history from its Holy fathers.
questions.answer: Patristic tradition

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Patristic tradition include?
questions.answer: Noble statuses based on the teaching of the Apostles with respect to the interpretation of the doctrines.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Several Christian denominations believe in sola scriptura, which translates to ______.
questions.answer: “By scripture alone” - means the bible contains all knowledge necessary for salvation and holiness, so nothing else should be used or supplemented with it (Our church, along with most Apostolic churches, do not agree with this theory).

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Bible is a compilation of books decided upon by which council?
questions.answer: Council of Carthage (AD 397)

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Where was the word ‘priest’ derived from?
questions.answer: It was derived from the Greek word ‘Presbyteros’ meaning “elder.”

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the role of a priest?
questions.answer: Acts as the mediator between God and man and the appointed teacher of religious truths and doctrines.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who was the term priesthood first attributed to?
questions.answer: Melchizedek

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Jesus Christ gave authority to his disciples to administer ______.
questions.answer:
1) Preach and Teach: Spreading the word of God
2) Power of Healing: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out demons
3) Perform the Holy Eucharist: Gave the mystery of his Holy Body and Precious Blood
4) Authority of Absolution & Binding of Sins: Christ gave his apostles the Holy Spirit and the authority to forgive and not forgive sins

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Which Saint is given the supremacy and primal position in the Holy Church?
questions.answer: St. Peter

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the priest’s role?
questions.answer:
1) Know his sheep: Understand the needs of his parishioners and care for them
2) Feed his sheep: Provide nourishment for his people through the Sacraments
3) Lead his sheep: Serves his people as a servant to a master
4) Protect his sheep
5) Train his sheep: Compliment good behaviors and correct wrongdoings
6) Seek lost sheep: The priest is hospitable to strangers and merciful to the poor, needy, and lost

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What happens when a priest is found to be in offense of unethical behaviors?
questions.answer: He is formally reprimanded and counseled by his bishop

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What disciplinary actions can a bishop make if a priest continues to defile the orders of the Church?
questions.answer: Restraining (Mudakku) for a short time, and excommunication (Mahron) as a last measure.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What roles do women play in the Church?
questions.answer: Teaching in Sunday School, being part of the spiritual organization and choir, mentoring others, involvement in charity work, and handling the administrative affairs of her parish.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Define matrimony.
questions.answer: The sacrament by which a baptized man and baptized woman bind themselves for life in a lawful marriage and receive the grace to discharge their duties.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the difference between a covenant and contract?
questions.answer: A covenant is an agreement that involves promises and commitments that are everlasting. A contract is a binding agreement focused on the exchange of goods, services, or property.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did God express through Prophet Hosea?
questions.answer: His everlasting love towards his people by forgiving adultery and by making them more faithful to him.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What type of bond is marriage?
questions.answer: A spiritual and holy bond.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What other reasons are there for marriage besides for the good and happiness of mankind?
questions.answer:
1) Procreation and furtherance of the human species
2) Guarding against sexual immorality
3) Help and assistance of one to another
4) Building block of a strong, stable, sound society

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the prerequisites to be married under the tradition of the Syriac Orthodox Church?
questions.answer:
1) The marriage should be officiated by a canonically ordained clergy
2) They should be of legal age to marry and mentally, physically, and emotionally stable
3) They should not have close blood relations
4) They should not have any other living life partner
5) They should receive the Sacraments of Holy Confession and Holy Qurbono
6) The couple should undergo premarital counseling

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How many parts does the marriage service have?
questions.answer: 2: Blessing of the Rings and Blessing of the Crown

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the rings symbolize?
questions.answer: A contract sealed and placed on the bride and groom by the priest.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Because a ring has no loose ends, what does this signify?
questions.answer: The permanency and the lifelong nature of the relationship expected from the couple.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Blessing of the Crowns signify?
questions.answer: The special grace of the Holy Spirit descending on the couple.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the “Crowns” represent?
questions.answer: The crown of thorns placed upon the head of Jesus Christ, who laid down his life for the Church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do the “Crowns” symbolize?
questions.answer: The glory, the reward, and the honor of God granted to the newly married couple.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the Sacramental items for marriage?
questions.answer: Minnu (Wedding Pendant) and Manthrakodi (Bridal Wedding Saree)

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the tying of the Minnu signify?
questions.answer: The couple is tied together for life.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Manthrakodi literally mean?
questions.answer: “Manthra” means bless and “kodi” means new clothes

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the draping of the bride with the bridal saree represent?
questions.answer: Rebecca, who took a veil and covered herself when she first saw Issac.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The relationship between who is an excellent model of the love and commitment between family members after marriage?
questions.answer: Ruth and Naomi

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is salvation?
questions.answer: The divine gift through which men and women are delivered from sin and death, united to Christ, and brought into his eternal kingdom.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How did Jesus achieve salvation?
questions.answer: Through his sacrifice.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is “deification?”
questions.answer: The lifelong process where the Holy Spirit helps us enjoy personal salvation and empowers us, rooted in the Holy Sacraments.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Holy Baptism?
questions.answer: The sacrament of entry into the Church, the body of Christ, and the first step to salvation.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: Only those who are baptized can partake in any of the other sacraments.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the Holy Church consider the baptismal font as?
questions.answer: The tomb of Jesus Christ and the womb of St. Mary.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the most important reason to anoint a baptized person with Mooron?
questions.answer: To give them rebirth in the Holy Spirit/

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Confirmation with this Holy Mooron is considered as ______?
questions.answer: A seal of true faith, acting as a cover to protect us from filth.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: Anointing of Holy Mooron on a believer is the process to give the first stage of royal priesthood.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does anointing of Holy Chrism represent?
questions.answer: The laying of hands by the Apostles on the believers.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How often should we participate in confession?
questions.answer: The Church encourages confession every 40 days, but there is a minimum requirement of once a year.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the book of the Acts of Apostles show?
questions.answer: The observance of Jesus’s commands by his disciples by devoting themselves to prayer and the breaking of bread.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the formula for salvation?
questions.answer: Faith in Christ + Keeping his Commands = Salvation

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the False Teachings about salvation?
questions.answer:
1) Salvation by Faith Alone
2) Confession by Own Mouth
3) Adult Baptism Only

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What was St. John’s baptism a symbol of?
questions.answer: An Old Testament practice of present baptism to prepare the Jews to receive the Messiah.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why does the Baptism of John never give absolution from sins?
questions.answer: According to St. Ivanius, the gold tongue, forgiveness of sin was only made possible after the redemptive acts of Jesus Christ.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is eschatology?
questions.answer: The “study of the last things”. It covers the study of events such as death, end of the world, resurrection of the dead, Second Coming of Jesus, judgment, heaven, and hell.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the difference between the terms ‘physical death’ and ‘departed’?
questions.answer: Physical death is the separation of soul from the body by the order of God. Departed expresses the idea that the soul is transferred from this world to another world.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What occurs to the soul immediately after death?
questions.answer: The soul is judged individually and remains until the Final Judgement, experiencing a foretaste of heaven or of hell.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the 4 states of a righteous soul?
questions.answer: The soul is alive and can see, hear, and talk.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who had prophesied the day of Jesus’ second coming and what events did they describe?
questions.answer: Prophet Joel said, “I will give warnings of that day in the sky and on earth; there will be bloodshed, fire, and clouds of smoke. The sun will be darkened, and the moon will turn red as blood before the great and terrible day of the Lord comes. But all who ask the Lord for help will be saved.”

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What do chapters 7-12 of Daniel detail?
questions.answer: Bloodshed and tribulations unequaled from the beginning of the world till now, and about final judgement day.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did Prophet Isaiah prophesize about?
questions.answer: The resurrection of the dead and the coming of God to give punishment to the inhabitants of earth for the iniquity and innocent bloodshed upon the earth.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did St. Paul warn the faithful about during Jesus’ second coming?
questions.answer: He warned the faithful about the Antichrist who will come with powerful signs, but assured them that the breath from the mouth of Jesus will destroy him.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: List the 6 events the Scripture mentions will occur before the second coming of Jesus.
questions.answer:
1) The preaching of the Gospel to all nations
2) The return of Israel to Christ
3) Coming of two witnesses
4) Appearance of Antichrist
5) A massive falling away from faith led by false prophets with powerful signs and wonders
6) Wars, revolutions, famine, and natural disasters

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why did God not tell us when Jesus will come again?
questions.answer: He wants us to be constantly prepared and to maintain constant purity in our lives.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What role does judgment play?
questions.answer: Judgment plays a vital role in God’s plan of salvation and is a central part of eternal life.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: Our church does not believe that the state of souls can be affected by the love and prayers of the righteous up until the Last Judgment?
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: On what days does the Church offer special prayers and Holy Qurbono for the departed people?
questions.answer: On the 3rd, 9th, 30th, and 40th days after death and on the anniversaries of their death.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Explain the false teachings on the second coming.
questions.answer:
1) Pre-tribulation rapture: Belief that all the “good Christians” will be raptured into heaven and escape from the trials and tribulations of the last days.
2) Millennialism: Belief that Christ will come back and establish a peaceful kingdom on Earth where he will reign with the “good Christians” for a thousand years.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Monasticism?
questions.answer: A religious way of life in which one renounces worldly pursuits to devote oneself fully to spiritual work.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What was the primary cause for the rise of Monasticism?
questions.answer: The laxness in the life of Christians after Christianity was declared legal and persecution ended.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did many people believe was the best way to serve Jesus?
questions.answer: Asceticism (self-discipline and abstinence from worldly pleasures) and detachment from mainstream society.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who is considered to be the first hermit (people who isolate themselves from society)?
questions.answer: St. Paul of Thebes, who led an eremitic life.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who is considered the father of monasticism?
questions.answer: St. Antony, since his holy life influenced many people, who submitted their lives to God and turned to a monastic life

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What command from the Lord did Ascetic Prophet Elijah follow?
questions.answer: He stayed on the bank of the river ‘Kerith’, east of Jordan, and the ravens brought him bread and meat morning and evening, and he drank from the stream.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the vows a monk must make to achieve Christian perfection and become perfect examples to others?
questions.answer:
1) Absolute obedience to his spiritual superior
2) Voluntary poverty, signifying that he may take nothing from the world as his personal property
3) Chastity, an oath to never marry and to remain chaste

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: When the Church faced hard times because of heretics or rulers, what did the monks do?
questions.answer: They abandoned their caves and went into the cities to help the faithful bear the oppression and to strengthen them in faith with their true teaching.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What was the importance of St. Antony, the father of Christian monks?
questions.answer: He empowered the faithful and clergy during the oppression by Maximinus; he visited Alexandria in 316 AD to comfort the faithful during the persecution; and he came back in 352 AD to help St. Athanasius in his fight against Arianism.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe the importance of St. Ephraim.
questions.answer: When famine broke out in Edessa, he visited wealthy people and collected alms from them and distributed them among the poor. When the plague broke out, he took care of and gave comfort to many victims of the plague.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Syriac Orthodox Church conforms to the teachings of which Ecumenical Councils?
questions.answer: The Councils of Nicea, Constantinople, and Ephesus.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Which council does the Syrian Orthodox Church reject?
questions.answer: The Council of Chalcedon

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: To what religion were Christians forced to convert?
questions.answer: Islam

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who founded Christianity in India?
questions.answer: St. Thomas

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: A very large group of Syriac Christians assembled and took a solemn oath by tying a long rope to a Cross in Mattancherry, denouncing the Portuguese (Roman) church leadership and accepting the leaders authorized by the Patriarch.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: The Cross bending due to the weight is known as ______.
questions.answer: Oath of the bent cross or the Coonan Cross Oath

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: The Chaldean Syrian Church of Kerala is not the only remnant of the Nestorian influence.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who introduced a false doctrine saying that Jesus existed as two persons, the human Jesus and divine Son of God?
questions.answer: Nestorius, the Patriarch of Constantinople

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: In 1774, the court separated the church belonging to Mor Kurilose from our Church and it became the Malabar Independent Church.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the doctrinal difference of the Protestant Church?
questions.answer: They disown St. Mary and all other saints, and they do not have prayers for the departed.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the doctrinal difference of the Malankara Catholic Church?
questions.answer: Some doctrinal differences exist, such as the filioque and the infallibility of the Pope.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the doctrinal difference of the Malankara Orthodox Church?
questions.answer: No doctrinal or main liturgical difference from our Church, though they have been excommunicated by our church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Who does the Malankara Orthodox Church consider as the head of the church instead of the Patriarch?
questions.answer: The Catholicose

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why is the Syriac Orthodox Church unique?
questions.answer:
1) Presents a form of Christianity that preserves the practices and preaching of the Apostolic times
2) Employs in its liturgy the Syriac language, an Aramaic dialect akin to the Aramaic spoken by Christ and the Apostles
3) Utilizes the most ancient liturgy handed from one generation to another
4) Demonstrates the unity of the body of Christ by the multiethnic nature of its faithful

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Explain the difference between spirituality in the Old Testament and New Testament.
questions.answer: The central aspect of Old Testament spirituality is the covenant relationship with God and the specially chosen people. The spirituality in the New Testament is Christian’s response to God’s saving initiative from generation to generation.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What did God promise through the covenant with Abraham in the Old Testament?
questions.answer: The redemption of whole nations through Abraham’s offspring.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Eastern spirituality based on?
questions.answer: The Holy Bible and traditional teachings of early Christian fathers, martyrs, ascetics, and saints.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is eternal life in Eastern spirituality?
questions.answer: A continuation of the spiritual life in this world.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does Eastern spirituality give more importance to?
questions.answer: The presence and work of the Holy Spirit.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is shared between the two branches of the Orthodox Church, Eastern Orthodox and Oriental Orthodox?
questions.answer: They have common beliefs, specifically in Trinitarian worship and similar liturgies.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the contrast in the spirituality of Eastern Orthodox churches and Oriental Orthodox Churches?
questions.answer: Eastern Orthodox Church believes that Jesus Christ was both fully human and fully divine. Oriental Orthodox Churches believe that Jesus is perfect in divinity and humanity, but that both natures were united in a single nature ‘without mingling, confusion, or alteration’.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: How does the Universal Syriac Orthodox Church identify spirituality?
questions.answer:
1) Holy Trinity Centered: We believe in the triune God because he revealed Himself to us as the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
2) Sacraments: The Church sees the sacraments as the therapeutic medicine for the soul’s transformation to eternal life.
3) Worship & Prayers: The Church gives more importance to worship and prayers on a community basis rather than an individual level.
4) Theology: The prayers and liturgy contain different faith aspects, ideologies, and respectful advice that let us learn the theology of the church.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are the ways to lead a Christian Spiritual Life?
questions.answer: Through prayer, scripture, lent & fasting & almsgiving, sacramental life, trust, remorse & reconciliation, spirituality in married life, serving others, virtues & beatitudes, sickness & death.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Describe three spiritual disciplines of the church that help to lead a life embedded in Christian spirituality.
questions.answer:
1) Prayer: To build spirituality, we must practice personal prayer, submitting our needs and sins before God privately, as well as practice family prayer to increase the bond and trust among the Church’s members.
2) Scripture: It is essential to read, hear, and learn the Word of God because it teaches us, rebukes us, corrects us, and instructs us in righteousness.
3) Lent, Fasting, & Almsgiving: Fasting is a demonstration of our sincerity in repentance and our willingness to live a godly life. We must also fast from worldly pleasures and give money, food, or anything we save to the needy.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Differentiate true remorse and reconciliation from confession.
questions.answer: Most people view confession as a sacrament to get purification so that they can receive Holy Communion, whereas true remorse leads us to reconcile with God and fellow beings.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: List three biblical references that illustrate the act of serving others is based on real Christian love?
questions.answer:
1) St. Paul served Christ with a whole heart and in true spirit.
2) St. Paul served with thankfulness and a clear conscience.
3) St. Paul advises us to offer our body as a living sacrifice to God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: List the beatitudes described by St. Mathew as essential tools to enhance Christian spirituality.
questions.answer: Humility, kindness, charity, chastity, temperance, patience, and diligence.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is Mourning a symbol of?
questions.answer: Repentance and suffering, resulting in comfort from God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What the ultimate aim of living a spiritual life?
questions.answer: To become closer to God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What are some social pressures that people face leading them to make poor choices?
questions.answer: Peer pressure, sibling pressure, parental pressure, and pressure from a boss, colleagues, or strangers.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: Why is it important that you be responsible in taking care of your bodies?
questions.answer: Our bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit, which we have received from God.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: You can escape the consequences of your actions, whether it's good or bad.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What can we do to escape temptation?
questions.answer:
1) Pray: Pray for guidance to be able to identify and overcome temptation
2) Spiritual Support System: Create a support system with people you trust and seek guidance from God
3) Abstinence: Stay away from ‘high-risk situations’ and learn to say no

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is wealth considered as generally?
questions.answer: The abundance of valuable resources or valuable material possessions.

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: List two Bible references to show that wealth comes from God.
questions.answer:
1) Philippians 4:19 says, “My God shall supply all your needs according to his riches in glory by Jesus Christ.”
2) 2 Corinthians 9:8 says, “God is able to make all grace abound toward you that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work.”

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: True or False: The word of God promises financial comfort and prosperity to a Christian.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What does the word of God promise a Christian?
questions.answer: “I know the plan I have for you, says the Lord, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope” (Jeremiah 29:11).

questions.section: Study Guide - Shy Thomas, Norcross, GA
questions.question: What is the true purpose of tithing?
questions.answer: To set aside our first and best resources to the Church, not out of obligation, but out of honesty and a sense of gratitude for all that God and his church as given to us.

questions.section: Synthesized
questions.question: What happened In 1994?
questions.answer: In 1994, the famous Syriac scholar, Very Rev. Kurian Arch Corepiscopos, translated the whole Peshita Bible from Syriac to Malayalam. The Syriac Orthodox Bible Society of India published this translation and named it 'Vishudha Grandham'; this is the official Bible of our Church.
questions.explanation: Synthesized from chapter 1: Development of Holy Scripture. Answer cited directly from text. Page reference corrected during review.

questions.section: Checkpoint
questions.question: What are the four sections of the Septuagint and what books make up each section?
questions.answer: The four sections of the Septuagint are: 1) Books of the Law: Genesis to Deuteronomy. 2) Books of Israel's History: Joshua to Maccabees. 3) Books of Wisdom: Psalms to Wisdom of Sirach. 4) Books of Prophecy: Hosea to Daniel.
questions.explanation: See chapter 2: Old Testament 1: Law & History
questions.source_excerpt: The books of the Septuagint have been divided into four sections from the time of creation to the exile of Israelites and their return thereafter. 1) Books of the Law: Genesis to Deuteronomy. 2) Books of Israel's History: Joshua to Maccabees. 3) Books of Wisdom: Psalms to Wisdom of Sirach. 4) Books...

questions.section: Challenge
questions.question: Women are also featured characters in the books of the Septuagint. Summarize either the book of Ruth, Esther or Judith.
questions.answer: Ruth: The book of Ruth tells of a Moabite woman who married into an Israelite family. After her husband died, she stayed with her mother-in-law Naomi, saying "Where you go I will go." Ruth later married Boaz and became an ancestor of King David and Jesus Christ. Esther: The book of Esther tells of a Jewish woman who became queen of Persia and saved her people from destruction plotted by Haman. Judith: The book of Judith tells of a brave woman who saved her city by defeating the Assyrian general Holofernes.
questions.explanation: See chapter 2: Old Testament 1: Law & History
questions.source_excerpt: Ruth, Esther, and Judith are featured in the Septuagint as women who showed courage and faith.

questions.section: Challenge
questions.question: Can you recite the 23 books that make up the Books of the Law and History?
questions.answer: 1) Books of the Law: Genesis to Deuteronomy. 2) Books of Israel's History: Joshua to Maccabees. The Law books include Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy. The History books include Joshua, Judges, Ruth, 1-4 Kings, 1-2 Chronicles, 1 Esdras, 2 Esdras, Nehemiah, Tobit, Judith, Esther, and 1-3 Maccabees.
questions.explanation: See chapter 2: Old Testament 1: Law & History
